volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 195 - Section 385)

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: ἅλιος . ἄλλαις δʼ ἄλλα προσέβα
sections.translation: One pain comes after another , to the house of the golden lamb . . . . from that earlier time when the Tantalids were killed ,

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: Τανταλιδᾶν ἐκβαίνει ποινά γʼ
sections.translation: punishment came to the house , and fate presses what you do not want upon you .

sections.label: Section 203
sections.original: ἐξ ἀρχᾶς μοι δυσδαίμων
sections.translation: From the beginning my fate was unhappy , from that first night of my mather ’ s marriage ;

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: καὶ νυκτὸς κείνας · ἐξ ἀρχᾶς
sections.translation: from the beginning the Fates attendant on my birth directed a hard upbringing for me , wooed by Hellenes , the first-born child in the home ,

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: Λήδας ἁ τλάμων κούρα
sections.translation: whom the unhappy daughter of Leda , by my father ’ s fault , bore as a victim and a sacrifice not joyful , she brought me up as an offering . In the horse-drawn chariot ,

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: ψαμάθων Αὐλίδος ἐπέβασαν
sections.translation: they set me as a bride on the sands of Aulis , oh woe , a wretched bride for the son of the Nereid , alas ! But now , as a stranger I live in an unfertile home on this sea that is hostile to strangers ,

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: ἄγαμος ἄτεκνος ἄπολις ἄφιλος ,
sections.translation: without marriage , or children , or city , or friends , not raising hymns to Hera at Argos , nor embroidering with my shuttle , in the singing loom , the likeness of Athenian Pallas and the Titans ; but

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: αἱμόρραντον δυσφόρμιγγα
sections.translation: . . . a bloody fate , not to be hymned by the lyre , of strangers who wail a piteous cry and weep piteous tears . And now I must forget these things ,

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: τὸν δʼ Ἄργει δμαθέντα κλαίω
sections.translation: and lament my brother , killed in Argos , whom I left at the breast , still a baby , still an infant , still a young child in his mother ’ s arms and at her breast ,

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: Ἄργει σκηπτοῦχον Ὀρέσταν .
sections.translation: the holder of the scepter in Argos , Orestes .

sections.label: Section 236
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὅδʼ ἀκτὰς ἐκλιπὼν θαλασσίους
sections.translation: Look , here comes a herdsman , who has left the shores of the sea to bring you some new message .

sections.label: Section 238
sections.original: Ἀγαμέμνονός τε καὶ Κλυταιμήστρας τέκνον ,
sections.translation: Daughter of Agamemnon , and of Clytemnestra , hear a strange report from me .

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι τοῦ παρόντος ἐκπλῆσσον λόγου ;
sections.translation: And what is amazing in your news ?

sections.label: Section 241
sections.original: ἥκουσιν ἐς γῆν , κυανέαν Συμπληγάδα
sections.translation: Two young men have come to this land , fleeing the dark Symplegades in their ship , an offering and sacrifice pleasing to the goddess Artemis . Be quick to prepare

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν φθάνοις ἂν εὐτρεπῆ ποιουμένη .
sections.translation: the purifications and the first offerings .

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: ποδαποί ; τίνος γῆς σχῆμʼ ἔχουσιν οἱ ξένοι ;
sections.translation: What country are the strangers from ? How are they dressed ?

sections.label: Section 247
sections.original: Ἕλληνες · ἓν τοῦτʼ οἶδα κοὐ περαιτέρω .
sections.translation: They are Hellenes ; I know this one thing , and nothing further .

sections.label: Section 248
sections.original: οὐδʼ ὄνομʼ ἀκούσας οἶσθα τῶν ξένων φράσαι ;
sections.translation: Can ’ t you tell me their names ? Did you hear them ?

sections.label: Section 249
sections.original: Πυλάδης ἐκλῄζεθʼ ἅτερος πρὸς θατέρου .
sections.translation: One was called Pylades by the other .

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: τοῦ ξυζύγου δὲ τοῦ ξένου τί τοὔνομʼ ἦν ;
sections.translation: What is the name of his companion ?

sections.label: Section 251
sections.original: οὐδεὶς τόδʼ οἶδεν · οὐ γὰρ εἰσηκούσαμεν .
sections.translation: No one knows ; we didn ’ t hear it .

sections.label: Section 252
sections.original: πῶς δʼ εἴδετʼ αὐτοὺς κἀντυχόντες εἵλετε ;
sections.translation: How did you see them ? How did you come upon them and catch them ?

sections.label: Section 253
sections.original: ἄκραις ἐπὶ ῥηγμῖσιν ἀξένου πόρου —
sections.translation: At the edge of the breakers of the Black Sea —

sections.label: Section 254
sections.original: καὶ τίς θαλάσσης βουκόλοις κοινωνία ;
sections.translation: And what do herdsmen have to do with the sea ?

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: βοῦς ἤλθομεν νίψοντες ἐναλίᾳ δρόσῳ .
sections.translation: We came to wash our cattle in the salt water .

sections.label: Section 256
sections.original: ἐκεῖσε δὴ ʼπάνελθε , πῶς νιν εἵλετε
sections.translation: Go back to the earlier question , how did you take them , and in what way , for I want to know this . They have come after a long time ; the altar of the goddess has not yet been reddened by streams of Hellene blood .

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: ἐπεὶ τὸν ἐσρέοντα διὰ Συμπληγάδων
sections.translation: When we were driving the cattle , that feed in the forest , into the sea that flows through the Symplegades , there was a broken cleft , hollowed by the constant surge of waves , shelter for those who hunt the purple-fish . Here one of the herdsmen saw two youths ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: βουφορβὸς ἡμῶν , κἀπεχώρησεν πάλιν
sections.translation: and made a retreat on tip-toe . He said : Don ’ t you see them ? These are deities that sit there . One of us , who revered the gods , lifted up his hands and prayed , as he saw them :

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: ὦ ποντίας παῖ Λευκοθέας , νεῶν φύλαξ ,
sections.translation: O son of the sea-goddess Leukothea , guardian of ships , lord Palaemon , be propitious to us ! Or do you sit on our shores , twin sons of Zeus ? Or the darlings of Nereus , father of the chorus of fifty Nereids ?

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: ἄλλος δέ τις μάταιος , ἀνομίᾳ θρασύς ,
sections.translation: Another , who was foolish and bold in his lawlessness , laughed at the prayers and asserted that ship-wrecked sailors were sitting on the cliff , in fear of our custom , having heard that we sacrifice strangers here .

sections.label: Section 279
sections.original: ἔδοξε δʼ ἡμῶν εὖ λέγειν τοῖς πλείοσι ,
sections.translation: Most of us thought that he spoke well ,

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: θηρᾶν τε τῇ θεῷ σφάγια τἀπιχώρια .
sections.translation: and that we ought to hunt down the customary offerings to the goddess . At this moment , one of the strangers left the rock , and stood , shaking his head up and down and groaning , with hands trembling , wandering in madness ; and like a hunter , he cried aloud :

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: Πυλάδη , δέδορκας τήνδε ; τήνδε δʼ οὐχ ὁρᾷς
sections.translation: Pylades , do you see her ? Don ’ t you see hell ’ s dragon , how she wants to kill me , fringed with her dreadful vipers against me ? and the one who breathes fire and slaughter from her robe and wings her way , my mother

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: ἔχουσα — πέτρινον ὄχθον , ὡς ἐπεμβάλῃ .
sections.translation: in her arms — the rocky mass , how she hurls it at me ! Ah , she will kill me ! Where can I escape ?

sections.label: Section 291a
sections.original: παρῆν δʼ ὁρᾶν
sections.translation: We could not see these shapes ; but he alternated the sounds of sheep and howling of dogs . . . to send forth the Furies ’ imitations .

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ συσταλέντες , ὡς θαμβούμενοι ,
sections.translation: Astonished , we cowered together and sat in silence ; but he drew his sword and rushed like a lion into the midst of our cattle , striking their flanks with his iron sword and thrusting it into their sides , thinking in this way to ward off the Furies ,

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: ὡς αἱματηρὸν πέλαγος ἐξανθεῖν ἁλός .
sections.translation: so that the waves of the sea blossomed red with blood . And all of us , as we saw our herds falling and ravaged , took arms , blew our conch shells , and collected the neighbors ; for we thought cowherds would make a poor fight of it

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: φαύλους μάχεσθαι βουκόλους ἡγούμεθα .
sections.translation: against well-grown and young foreigners . After a long time , our numbers were complete . But the stranger fell down , his pulsing beat of madness gone , his chin dripping with foam ; when we saw him , so conveniently fallen , each of us went to work ,

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: βάλλων ἀράσσων . ἅτερος δὲ τοῖν ξένοιν
sections.translation: hurling and striking at him . The other stranger wiped off the foam and tended his body , covering him with a finely-woven robe , looking out for the attacking blows , treating his friend kindly with his care .

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: ἔμφρων δʼ ἀνᾴξας ὁ ξένος πεσήματος
sections.translation: The stranger , now in his senses , started up from his fall and realized the surge of enemies close at hand and the present danger to them both , with a groan ; we did not let up our attack with stones , pressing hard from all sides .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: οὗ δὴ τὸ δεινὸν παρακέλευσμʼ ἠκούσαμεν ·
sections.translation: Then we heard his dreadful exhortation : Pylades , we shall die , but let us die with glory ; draw your sword , and follow me .

sections.label: Section 323
sections.original: ὡς δʼ εἴδομεν δίπαλτα πολεμίων ξίφη ,
sections.translation: But when we saw our enemies brandishing their two swords , we fled and filled up the rocky glens .

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , εἰ φύγοι τις , ἅτεροι προσκείμενοι
sections.translation: But while some would flee , others pressed on and attacked them ; if they drove those back , the ones who had just given way struck them with stones again . But it was hard to believe ; with so many hands , no one succeeded in hitting these offerings to the goddess .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: μόλις δέ νιν τόλμῃ μὲν οὐ χειρούμεθα ,
sections.translation: We got the better of them with difficulty ; not by daring , but by surrounding them in a circle , with stones we took away their swords ; they sank on their knees to the ground , in weariness . Then we brought them to the lord of this land . He saw them , and at once

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: ἐς χέρνιβάς τε καὶ σφαγεῖʼ ἔπεμπέ σοι .
sections.translation: sent them to you , for purification and slaughter . You have prayed for such sacrificial victims as these strangers , lady ; if you destroy them , Hellas will make atonement for your murder and pay the penalty for the sacrifice in Aulis .

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: θαυμάστʼ ἔλεξας τὸν μανένθʼ , ὅστις ποτὲ
sections.translation: You have told an amazing story about this madman , whoever he is , who has come from Hellas to the Black Sea .

sections.label: Section 342
sections.original: εἶἑν · σὺ μὲν κόμιζε τοὺς ξένους μολών ,
sections.translation: Very well . You go and bring the strangers here ; the holy rites will be my concern . Exit Herdsman .

sections.label: Section 344
sections.original: ὦ καρδία τάλαινα , πρὶν μὲν ἐς ξένους
sections.translation: O my unhappy heart , you were gentle to strangers before ,

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: γαληνὸς ἦσθα καὶ φιλοικτίρμων ἀεί ,
sections.translation: and always full of pity , measuring out tears for the sake of our common race , whenever Hellenes came into your hands . But now , after those dreams that have made me savage , thinking that Orestes is no longer alive ,

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: δύσνουν με λήψεσθʼ , οἵτινές ποθʼ ἥκετε .
sections.translation: whoever comes here will find me harsh to them . This is true after all , my friends , I have realized : the unfortunate , when themselves doing badly , do not have kind thoughts towards those who are more unfortunate .

sections.label: Section 354
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔτε πνεῦμα Διόθεν ἦλθε πώποτε ,
sections.translation: But no breeze from Zeus ever came ,

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: οὐ πορθμίς , ἥτις διὰ πέτρας Συμπληγάδας
sections.translation: or a boat , bringing Helen here , through the rocks of the Symplegedes — Helen who destroyed me , with Menelaus , so that I might avenge myself on them , setting an Aulis here against that one there , where the Danaids overpowered me and were going to sacrifice me like a calf ,

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: ἔσφαζον , ἱερεὺς δʼ ἦν ὁ γεννήσας πατήρ .
sections.translation: and my own father was the priest .

sections.label: Section 361
sections.original: οἴμοι — κακῶν γὰρ τῶν τότʼ οὐκ ἀμνημονῶ —
sections.translation: Ah me !— I cannot forget those past evils — how often did I stroke my father ’ s cheek and , hanging on his knees , told him : O father , I am brought

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: νυμφεύματʼ αἰσχρὰ πρὸς σέθεν · μήτηρ δʼ ἐμὲ
sections.translation: to a shameful betrothal by you ; but while you are killing me , my mother and the Argive women are singing wedding hymns , and the whole house is filled with the music of flutes ; but I am being destroyed by you . For Achilles was Hades after all , not the son of Peleus ,

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: ὅν μοι προσείσας πόσιν , ἐν ἁρμάτων ὄχοις
sections.translation: whom you held out to me as a husband , and you brought me in a chariot to a bloody wedding by treachery . But I was modestly looking out through a fine veil , and did not take up my brother in my arms — and now he is dead — did not kiss my sister ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: συνῆψʼ ὑπʼ αἰδοῦς , ὡς ἰοῦσʼ ἐς Πηλέως
sections.translation: because I was going to the house of Peleus ; I put off many embraces to another time , thinking that I would come back again to Argos .

sections.label: Section 378
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , εἰ τέθνηκας , ἐξ οἵων καλῶν
sections.translation: My unhappy Orestes , if you are dead , what glories have you left , what achievements of a father !

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: τὰ τῆς θεοῦ δὲ μέμφομαι σοφίσματα ,
sections.translation: I blame the goddess ’ subtleties ; whichever mortal has engaged in murder , or has touched a woman in childbirth or a corpse , she drives from her altars , thinking him impure ; but she herself delights in human sacrifices .

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: οὐκ ἔσθʼ ὅπως ἔτεκεν ἂν ἡ Διὸς δάμαρ
sections.translation: It is not possible that Leto , the wife of Zeus , gave birth to such folly . I judge that the feast prepared by Tantalus for the gods is not to be believed , that they fed on the flesh of his son ; and I think that the people here , who are themselves killers of men ,
